topology.currentShape: Manager HTTP API plus Worker RabbitMQ consumer
topology.targetShape: Importable Fetcher Engine with current Manager and Worker retained as optional compatibility adapters
topology.sourceOfTruth: go.mod for Go toolchain version; docs/PROJECT_RULES.md for architecture rules unless a dev-cycle reconciliation task updates it

inputs.authBoundary: Engine core keeps auth outside; current Manager adapter keeps lib-auth middleware
inputs.licenseBoundary: Engine core keeps license outside; current app adapters keep lib-license middleware
inputs.apiStandards: camelCase embedded contracts; current API already uses JSON-style camelCase
inputs.dbNaming: logical field names in docs; persistence adapters follow backing-store conventions

decisions: Treat the work as a strangler extraction, not greenfield development.
Create an importable Engine composition root from reusable service internals.
Keep operational shell concerns in host apps and compatibility adapters.
Do not make RabbitMQ, MongoDB, Redis, Fiber, Docker Compose, KEDA, S3, or SeaweedFS mandatory Engine core dependencies.
Reference existing docs/PROJECT_RULES.md as the architecture source of truth; do not overwrite it.
Use pkg/engine as the importable Engine core package and keep optional compatibility wrappers outside core under pkg/enginecompat/*.

Use go.mod as the Go toolchain source of truth. It declares Go 1.25.9, and dev-cycle must reconcile docs or tooling before code implementation starts without this pre-dev pass modifying docs/PROJECT_RULES.md.
Preserve plugin_crm as adapter-level compatibility behavior for the first Engine release only. Do not promote plugin_crm into a generic Engine core extension yet.
openFollowUps: Gate 9 delivery planning was not requested and remains NOT_REQUESTED.
